assertions.text: The vehicle was making a right turn onto a southbound road.
The vehicle had just accelerated from a near stop immediately before the collision.
Another car struck the left side of the Outback.
There was a single impact between the Outback and the other car.
The collision occurred during the right-turn maneuver onto the southbound road.
Severity is not explicitly stated (no claim of minor or severe damage).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows steering angle changing from [220.0 deg at t=-5.0 s](T2) to [22.5 deg at t=0.0 s](T1), indicating a steering maneuver, but device axes alignment to cardinal directions (north/south) or mapping steering sign to a right turn is not documented, so whether it was a right turn onto a southbound road is unverifiable.

Speed increased from [6.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s] (T2) to [28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s] (T4) and accelerator pedal rose to [22.0% at t=-1.0 s] then dropped to [0.0% at t=-0.5 s] (T2), indicating the vehicle accelerated from near-stop immediately before impact.

The crash pulse shows lateral cumulative delta-V peaking at [4.0 km/h at t=0.07 s] and longitudinal cumulative delta-V reaching [−17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s] (T3); the larger magnitude longitudinal negative delta-V indicates a primary rear/forward-directed impulse rather than a dominant left-side lateral impact on the vehicle; the telemetry does not show a lateral delta-V consistent with a left-side strike, thus the specific claim that another car struck the left side is contradicted.

CISS delta-V curves show a single crash pulse with longitudinal delta-V evolving smoothly to [−17.0 km/h by t=0.19 s] and lateral delta-V rising once to [4.0 km/h by t=0.07 s] then holding (T3), consistent with a single impact event recorded.

While steering and speed traces show a turning maneuver (steering at [17.5 deg at t=-0.5 s] and [22.5 deg at t=0.0 s] and speed near [28.0 km/h at t=0.0 s]) (T2,T4), telemetry cannot determine the road heading (southbound) or whether the collision occurred during the right-turn maneuver specifically, so this assertion is unverifiable.

The narrative makes no explicit quantitative statement about damage severity; telemetry EDR summary shows max delta-V longitudinal magnitude [17.0 km/h] and driver airbag stage triggered at [45.0 ms] (T1), but the assertion that severity is not stated is a statement about the narrative content and is supported (no severity claim present).

result.rationale: Telemetry supports that the vehicle accelerated from near-stop and that a single impact occurred, and shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V of [−17.0 km/h at t=0.19 s] (T3), which contradicts a clear left-side strike; however steering/heading and roadway orientation are not determinable from the device-frame data, so several assertions about turn direction and road heading remain unverifiable.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

accel_pedal.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned
